applicability.sex_or_reproductive_context: patient with ovarian/uterine or pregnancy-capable reproductive physiology relevant to the complaint
use_when: Use when menstrual, ovulatory, androgen-excess, ovarian insufficiency, menopause, or pregnancy-exclusion context changes testing and counseling.
Use when local history confirms the reproductive physiology or anatomy relevant to the selected module.
do_not_use_when: Do not use as a generic endocrine workup when menstrual or ovarian physiology is not clinically applicable.
Do not use when pregnancy, postpartum state, prior hysterectomy/oophorectomy, or gender-affirming care changes the appropriate pathway without documenting that modifier.

requiredExam.label: Inspect acne distribution

source.source_section: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Inflammatory acne supports hyperandrogenism and changes androgen testing threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When androgen excess or PCOS is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Inflammatory acne supports hyperandrogenism and changes androgen testing threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect acne distribution is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Inspect terminal hair distribution

requiredExam.label: Inspect thyroid gland

source.source_section: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern. Goiter changes thyroid testing interpretation and differential diagnosis.
requiredExam.rationale: Looks for localizing or mass-effect findings that change imaging, specialty escalation, or procedure planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the thyroid from in front or behind while the patient swallows, noting size, tenderness, asymmetry, nodules, fixation, or bruit if auscultated.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When menstrual, fertility, or menopausal symptoms could reflect thyroid disease
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: thyroid structural disease, compressive features, or thyroid inflammatory/autoimmune pattern
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Goiter changes thyroid testing interpretation and differential diagnosis.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect thyroid gland is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique. Pair this technique-dependent finding with the relevant labs, imaging, serial trend, or specialty exam before changing definitive therapy.

requiredExam.technique: Inspect for spontaneous discharge and, only when clinically appropriate, gently express from the areola toward the nipple while preserving privacy and consent.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When amenorrhea, infertility, breast symptoms, or prolactin excess is possible

requiredExam.limitations: Inspect for galactorrhea is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure

source.source_section: Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency curated endocrine workup: basic bedside data and safety checks
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.

requiredExam.management_change: Tachycardia, bradycardia, or irregular rhythm changes acuity, ECG need, and treatment urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity.
requiredExam.rationale: Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.

source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when virilization, rapid, androgen, hirsutism is present; this evaluates gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype. Virilization changes urgency of androgen tumor evaluation.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect the relevant androgen-sensitive areas respectfully and document distribution, severity, and signs of virilization.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When rapid virilization or severe androgen excess is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: gonadal axis, prolactin, androgen excess/deficiency, or reproductive endocrine phenotype
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Virilization changes urgency of androgen tumor evaluation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect for clitoromegaly is supportive for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter.
Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.
IGF-1, LH/FSH, estradiol, AMH, and pediatric growth labs require age/sex/puberty-specific ranges.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: For typical menopause at usual age, diagnose clinically when appropriate; rule out pregnancy or abnormal bleeding causes when indicated.
For POI under age 40, confirm ovarian insufficiency with elevated FSH and low estradiol in the right clinical context, repeating if uncertain.
Evaluate POI causes and consequences with pregnancy test, TSH/prolactin when relevant, karyotype/FMR1/adrenal-thyroid autoimmunity, DXA, and fertility counseling as indicated.
Assess contraindications to hormone therapy, cardiovascular/VTE/breast cancer risk, uterine status, symptom burden, bone risk, and patient preferences.
Confirm that Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: Pregnancy test if amenorrhea; FSH and estradiol for POI; FSH commonly >25 IU/L in context, repeat if uncertainty; TSH/prolactin.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: Prolactin upper limit often about 20 ng/mL in men and 25 ng/mL in women, but lab, pregnancy, lactation, and macroprolactin matter. Male total testosterone should be early morning and repeated; lower limit often around 264-300 ng/dL depending assay.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Use menopausal hormone therapy for bothersome vasomotor symptoms or POI replacement when benefits outweigh risks and contraindications are absent.
For POI, generally replace estrogen to the usual age of menopause with progestogen if uterus is present, plus bone/cardiovascular and fertility counseling.
Use nonhormonal options for vasomotor symptoms when hormone therapy is contraindicated or declined.
Treat genitourinary symptoms with local vaginal therapy or nonhormonal measures based on severity, contraindications, and preference.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.

For confirmed Menopause / Premature Ovarian Insufficiency, choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
